outcome_contract.why: Cleaning staff have no way to signal when a room is done. Reception staff checking guests in are flying blind on whether the room is actually ready. Fixing this means both teams can do their jobs without phone calls or guesswork.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Cleaning staff can mark individual rooms as cleaned on the prepare dashboard. The checkins screen shows a per-room cleanliness indicator. Reception staff can see at a glance whether a room is ready before a guest reaches the desk.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Mark-clean button in `CleaningPriorityTable`; cleanliness pill in `view/BookingRow`; 8th column + header update
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Both UI surfaces required; use `Chip` pattern from `CleaningPriorityTable`; pill uses `bg-success-main`/`bg-error-main` semantic tokens; 8th `TableHeader` column + `colSpan` update

engineering_coverage.applicable: Loading state on button (disabled while pending); error toast on failure; already-clean state
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Optimistic update rejected — await Firebase write then let subscription refresh render; `showToast` on error; button disabled during write

engineering_coverage.applicable: Same Firebase auth as all reception staff; no PII in room status
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: N/A — all logged-in reception staff can write; acceptable

engineering_coverage.applicable: `saveRoomStatus` only records error state + rethrows; new button path needs explicit `try/catch` + `showToast`
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: `try/catch` + `showToast("Failed to mark room as clean", "error")` required in `PrepareDashboard` handler

engineering_coverage.applicable: Tests across 5 files: button interaction + mock update (`PrepareDashboard.test.tsx`), guard unit (`usePrepareDashboard.test.ts`), cleanliness pill + prop-threading (`BookingRow.test.tsx`, `CheckinsTable.test.tsx`), 8th column header snapshot (`TableHeader.test.tsx`); `CheckinsTable.test.tsx` mocks `TableHeader` so the header column change is covered by the separate `TableHeader.test.tsx` snapshot suite
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: 5 files impacted; `TableHeader.test.tsx` snapshot must be updated separately from `CheckinsTable.test.tsx`

engineering_coverage.applicable: `SingleRoomStatus` schema unchanged; `saveRoomStatus` existing mutation; guard uses `toEpochMillis` (not `isToday`)
Single subscription at `CheckinsTable` level; prop-thread `roomStatusMap` through `CheckinsTableView → BookingRow`; ~10 rooms = small dataset
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: No per-row subscriptions; `useRoomStatusMutations()` called once in `PrepareDashboard`; `roomStatusMap` exposed from `usePrepareDashboardData()` return (no duplicate listener)

engineering_coverage.applicable: Cloudflare Worker hot-swap; no DB migration; Firebase writes idempotent
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: No feature flag; deploy + revert is sufficient rollback

stage_payload.decision_summary: Two independent screens need to be connected via a shared Firebase data path that already exists but has no write surface and no read surface in the UI. The core decision is whether to deliver both ends of the pipeline together (mark-clean write + checkins read) or split delivery. A secondary decision is the exact wiring architecture for the subscription data flow.
goals: Cleaning staff can mark a room as cleaned on the prepare dashboard
The mark-clean signal survives page reload and is never silently overwritten for occupied rooms
Reception staff see a per-room cleanliness pill on the checkins screen
No new Firebase paths or npm dependencies
constraints: `SingleRoomStatus` Zod schema must remain backward compatible
`/roomStatus/index_<room>` is the write target — no new paths
UI must use reception design system tokens (Tailwind v4 semantic tokens)
assumptions: `roomAllocated` in `CheckInRow` holds raw room number (e.g. "3") → Firebase key `index_3`
`useFirebaseSubscription` creates one `onValue` listener per hook instance — no React-layer deduplication
`usePrepareDashboardData()` already subscribes to `/roomStatus` internally; exposing `roomStatusMap` in its return value adds zero subscription overhead

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Both staff roles must benefit from one delivery; a half-delivery leaves the workflow broken
Per-row `onValue` listeners are duplicate work on the same Firebase connection (not separate WebSocket channels), but still wasteful; a single shared subscription is the correct pattern
Stayover cleans (occupied room) must survive page reload; the guard is the correctness hinge
Reception uses Tailwind v4 semantic tokens; new elements must use `bg-success-main`, `bg-error-main` etc.
New interactive paths need unit test coverage per testing policy
Simpler is better when both options deliver the goal

options_considered.Description: Mark-clean button on prepare dashboard, auto-sync guard fix, per-room cleanliness pill on checkins
options_considered.Upside: Completes the entire staff workflow; both roles get value in one deploy
options_considered.Key risks: Slightly wider scope, but all changes are additive

options_considered.Description: Mark-clean button + guard fix; no change to checkins screen
options_considered.Downside: Reception staff still need to navigate to prepare dashboard manually; the stated goal ("see at a glance on checkins") is not met
options_considered.Key risks: Partial delivery undercuts the operator intent

options_considered.Description: Read-only pill on checkins derived from existing auto-sync data
options_considered.Downside: No write path → pill always shows "Dirty" for occupied rooms regardless of actual cleaning; stayover cleans can never be signalled
options_considered.Key risks: Delivers a widget with misleading data

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A — full end-to-end pipeline in a single delivery
chosen_approach.Why this wins: The operator intent is explicit: reception staff must be able to see room readiness on the checkins screen. Options B and C each deliver one half of a pipeline that only works when both ends are live. Option A is the minimum complete delivery. The scope is bounded (6–8 files, all additive changes). No new Firebase paths or mutations are needed; a new `useRoomStatusData()` subscription is added at the checkins side, but the hook and the Firebase path (`/roomStatus`) already exist — this is a new usage, not new infrastructure.
chosen_approach.Mark Clean button availability: Button is **today-only** — disabled (or hidden) when `selectedDate !== localToday`. `/roomStatus/index_<room>` is a live room-level record with **no date partition**; writing from a historical or future date view would overwrite the current-day live readiness state seen on both the prepare dashboard and the checkins screen. Planning must gate the button on `isToday(selectedDate)`, consistent with how the auto-sync effect already conditions on `isTodayDate(selectedDate)` at line ~360 of `usePrepareDashboard.ts`.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (prepare-only)** — Only solves the write side; reception staff still fly blind on the checkins screen. Half-delivery contradicts the stated outcome.
**Option C (checkins-only)** — Read surface without write path shows "Dirty" for stayover rooms regardless of actual cleaning. Delivers a misleading widget.

validation_implications: All tests run in CI only (testing policy); validate by pushing to dev branch

TypeScript typecheck must pass — `usePrepareDashboardData()` returns an inferred object (no explicit interface); adding `roomStatusMap` is additive; the primary seam is the mock in `PrepareDashboard.test.tsx` which hardcodes the hook's return shape and must be updated alongside TASK-01
sequencing_constraints: TASK-01 and TASK-02 must precede TASK-03 (guard must be in place before button lands)
TASK-04 must precede TASK-05 (subscription must be in place before pill reads from it)
TASK-06 can run in parallel with TASK-04/05 for prepare-side tests; checkins tests after TASK-05

areas.label: Cleaning staff workflow
areas.current: Read-only table; no way to mark room as cleaned
areas.trigger: Cleaning staff finishes cleaning a room
areas.future: Staff opens prepare dashboard → sees per-room table → clicks "Mark Clean" button → button disables during write → Firebase `/roomStatus/index_<room>` updated with `{ clean: "Yes", cleaned: <ISO timestamp> }` → table chip refreshes to "Clean" in real-time
areas.steady: Cleanliness table, occupancy/checkout data columns, date selector
areas.seams: Error path: write failure shows `showToast` error; room chip stays "Dirty"

areas.label: Auto-sync (stayover guard)
areas.current: Auto-sync unconditionally overwrites `clean` based on occupancy; no guard for already-manually-cleaned rooms
areas.future: Auto-sync checks: if `clean === "Yes" && typeof cleaned === "string" && toEpochMillis(cleaned) >= todayTime` → skip dirty overwrite; manual clean survives until midnight
areas.steady: Auto-sync still runs and handles initial dirty detection for all rooms
areas.seams: Guard uses same epoch-compare pattern as existing `mergedData` at line ~315; string narrowing required before `toEpochMillis()` call

areas.label: Reception checkins view
areas.current: No per-room status; global "Rooms Ready" toggle only
areas.future: Reception staff opens checkins view → per-room "Clean?" pill visible in new 8th column for each booking row → green "Clean" / red "Dirty" derived from `/roomStatus/index_<roomAllocated>` → single Firebase subscription at `CheckinsTable` level; all rows read from shared `roomStatusMap` prop
areas.steady: Global "Rooms Ready" toggle unchanged; all other booking row columns unchanged
areas.seams: `roomAllocated` may be `undefined` for some bookings — pill must render nothing (graceful empty); notes-row `colSpan` must be updated from 7 to 8
